scheme.name: Moss
scheme.headline: Blue-yellow-green material color language
scheme.summary: A crisp blue-yellow-green material palette for code interfaces.

ground.description: Primary charcoal substrate with almost no visible haze.

carrier.description: Readable parchment carrier neutral for body text and long-lived structure.

shell.description: Raised shell family for layered rails, grouped chrome, and side housing.

slate.description: Warm gray-slate neutral for comments and low-pressure guidance.

spark.description: Clear old-warning yellow family for control flow and directive pressure without Ember-like orange heat.

amber.description: Burnished brass and lacquered-paper family for strings, measured highlights, and dry straw warmth.

voltage.description: Oxidized CRT-blue family for constants and secondary structural jump points.

citron.description: Secondary ochre family for operators, warm separators, and cleaner mustard-metal edges.

jade.description: Terminal lichen, sage, and oxidized patina family for green callable rhythm and blue structural type relief.

chalk.description: Parchment neutral carrier for variables, properties, and parameters, keeping the page rhythmic.

panel.description: Raised document substrate mixed up from canvas toward ink.

gutter.description: Visible but quiet gutter band tuned for faster line scanning.

sidebar.description: Shell anchor for navigation, file rails, and grouped UI housing.

border.description: Sharper structural edge than Hearth, but still subordinate to syntax landmarks.

cursor.description: Primary live cursor and active relief signal.

status.description: Persistent emphasis band used for status presence and release accents.

focusRing.description: Focusable edge signal used for ring and border emphasis.

lineEmphasis.description: Current-line substrate emphasis without becoming a spotlight.

listHover.description: Hover substrate for navigational lists and tabs.
